explanation: The requirement specifies that whenever 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the previous ventilation session. We have atomic propositions 'resumeVentilation' for the trigger (selection of Resume Ventilation) and 'loadLastParams' for the required response (loading previous parameters). This corresponds to the classic response pattern in LTL, captured by the template 'G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)', meaning 'Globally, if resumeVentilation becomes true, then loadLastParams must also become true.' This guarantees that whenever Resume Ventilation is selected, the relevant parameters are loaded as required by the specification.

The input natural language describes a requirement that, whenever the 'Resume Ventilation' option is selected, the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters associated with the last active patient ventilation. In the set of atomic propositions, 'resumeVentilation' indicates that 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, and 'loadLastParams' reflects that the system loads the last calibration/alarm/ventilation parameters. The correct temporal relationship is that every time 'resumeVentilation' is true, 'loadLastParams' should follow (i.e., occur in the next moment or at the same time). Therefore, we use the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', with bool_exp1='resumeVentilation' and bool_exp3='loadLastParams'. This ensures that whenever resume ventilation is selected at any time in the system’s operation, the required loading action is carried out.

The requirement states that whenever 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the system must load the saved calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation. The atomic proposition 'resumeVentilation' indicates that 'Resume Ventilation' has been selected, and 'loadLastParams' corresponds to the action of loading those last settings. The most direct way to model this causal relationship in LTL is to say that globally, if 'resumeVentilation' is true, 'loadLastParams' must also be true. This captures the requirement that selecting 'Resume Ventilation' always leads (in the same step or immediately after) to loading the relevant parameters.

The requirement states that if the 'Resume Ventilation' option is selected, the system must load the last saved calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters. These are succinctly represented by the atomic propositions: 'resumeVentilation' (Resume Ventilation selected) and 'loadLastParams' (load last parameters). The implication is that whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true, it should guarantee 'loadLastParams' is also true, and this should hold globally (invariant) within the operation. The appropriate template is: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), corresponding to template ID 30. Here, bool_exp1 is 'resumeVentilation', bool_exp3 is 'loadLastParams', and other placeholders are left empty as the template doesn't use them. This LTL property precisely captures the described requirement.

The requirement states that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation. The most direct mapping to atomic propositions uses 'resumeVentilation' to indicate the trigger, and 'loadLastParams' to indicate the required system action. Thus, the LTL property should specify that whenever 'resumeVentilation' becomes true, 'loadLastParams' must follow. Template 11 captures the idea that upon 'resumeVentilation', in the next state, 'loadLastParams' becomes true, which fits the intent that the system loads the parameters when resuming. N_DURATION is not required for template 11, so it is omitted.

The input requirement states that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, then the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation. The atomic propositions available include 'resumeVentilation' (indicates Resume Ventilation selected) and 'loadLastParams' (indicates loading the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation).

Template 30 (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)) is appropriate because it expresses that globally, whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true (i.e., Resume Ventilation is selected), the property 'loadLastParams' (loading the parameters) must hold as a response. This template straightforwardly captures the cause (resumeVentilation) and effect (loadLastParams) described in the natural language requirement.

The requirement specifies that when 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters. The atomic proposition 'resumeVentilation' corresponds to the selection of the Resume Ventilation option, and 'loadLastParams' corresponds to loading the appropriate parameters. Therefore, we select a template for 'globally, if resumeVentilation then loadLastParams'. This ensures that at any point in time, whenever the Resume Ventilation is selected, the system is required to perform the loading of the previous parameters as described.

The natural language requirement states: if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation. Among the provided atomic propositions, 'resumeVentilation' directly indicates the selection of 'Resume Ventilation,' and 'loadLastParams' explicitly refers to loading the correct parameters as described. This means whenever 'resumeVentilation' becomes true (i.e., is selected), 'loadLastParams' should immediately become true (i.e., the system should load the required parameters). This describes a response relationship, appropriately captured by the 'Globally, if bool_exp2, then next bool_exp3 holds' template (ID 11). Thus, this selection ensures that on every occurrence of 'resumeVentilation,' in the next step, 'loadLastParams' will be true, directly encoding the specified requirement.

The requirement is: if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, then the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation. This is directly modeled by saying: globally, whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true (i.e., Resume Ventilation is selected), it must follow that 'loadLastParams' becomes true (i.e., the system loads the previous parameters). This is a safety property that restricts the system to always perform the loading action upon user selection, and is perfectly captured by template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 is 'resumeVentilation' and bool_exp3 is 'loadLastParams'.

The requirement states that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, the system must load the last calibration parameters, alarm thresholds, and ventilation parameters from the last active patient ventilation. Here, 'resumeVentilation' indicates selection of the Resume Ventilation button, and 'loadLastParams' indicates the system loading the required parameters. The specification is a causality requirement that is well captured by the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', i.e., globally, whenever 'resumeVentilation' is true, eventually 'loadLastParams' must also become true. Thus, for bool_exp1, I use 'resumeVentilation' (not left empty) and for bool_exp3, 'loadLastParams'.
